TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == bacu == / izaw ku bacu nina kilang. siwkay nu bacu bacu sa, napatuzu' ku zuma a kawaw masaheciay saca canacanan a tuutuud. bacu sa kapah tu ku nu sulit kulit suni saca kawih nu lima, pasubana' tu tawan, u canan ku pakatineng nu tademaw. u bacu nu lacul hakay yadah ku patinengay. u ayzaay a subal nu binawlan, yadah ku bacu, caykalecad ku kuma, malecad ku bacu, sisa izaw ku bacu matahkal i kitakitakit. kya matineng ku caykalecad kamuay a tademaw, u canan ku pasubana'ay. u bacu a bana'-kakuziza , u tatengaay a bana' tuway. katuuday ku minanamay. mapabacu mapabacu Ś∑≤ŚĀöŤ®ėŤôü/ mipabacu tu ku luma' nu tumuk. mipabacu mipabacu ŚĀöŤ®ėŤôü/ mipabacu kaku tu laed nu umah. pabacuan pabacuan ŤĘęś®ôÁ§ļŤ®ėŤôüÁöĄŚúįśĖĻ/ pabacuan tu ku luma' ni Kacaw. sibacuay sibacuay śúČŤ®ėŤôüÁöĄ/ katinengan ku sibacuay ku badahung a luma'. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika A sign is an object, quality, event, or entity whose presence or occurrence indicates the probable presence or occurrence of something else. A natural sign bears a causal relation to its object‚ÄĒfor instance, thunder is a sign of storm, or medical symptoms signify a disease. A conventional sign signifies by agreement, as a full stop signifies the end of a sentence; similarly the words and expressions of a language, as well as bodily gestures, can be regarded as signs, expressing particular meanings. The physical objects most commonly referred to as signs (notices, road signs, etc., collectively known as signage) generally inform or instruct using written text, symbols, pictures or a combination of these. The philosophical study of signs and symbols is called semiotics; this includes the study of semiosis, which is the way in which signs operate.
S.PHON: == ba ¶u == / izaw ku ba ¶u nina kir~…ĺaŇč. siwkaj nu ba ¶u ba ¶u sa, napatuzu Ē~ ° ku zuma a kawaw masańß…ô ¶iaj sa ¶a ¶ana ¶anan a tuutuud. ba ¶u sa kapańß tu ku nu sur~…ĺit kur~…ĺit suni sa ¶a kawińß nu r~…ĺima, pasubana Ē~ ° tu tawan, u ¶anan ku pakatin…ôŇč nu tad…ômaw. u ba ¶u nu r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ ńßakaj jadańß ku patin…ôŇčaj. u ajzaaj a subar~…ĺ nu binawr~…ĺan, jadańß ku ba ¶u, ¶ajkar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad ku kuma, mar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad ku ba ¶u, sisa izaw ku ba ¶u matańßkar~…ĺ i kitakitakit. kja matin…ôŇč ku ¶ajkar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad kamuaj a tad…ômaw, u ¶anan ku pasubana Ē~ °aj. u ba ¶u a bana Ē~ °-kakuziza , u tat…ôŇčaaj a bana Ē~ ° tuwaj. katuudaj ku minanamaj. mapaba ¶u mapaba ¶u ****/ mipaba ¶u tu ku r~…ĺuma Ē~ ° nu tumuk. mipaba ¶u mipaba ¶u ***/ mipaba ¶u kaku tu r~…ĺa…ôd nu umańß. paba ¶uan paba ¶uan ********/ paba ¶uan tu ku r~…ĺuma Ē~ ° ni ka ¶aw. siba ¶uaj siba ¶uaj ****/ katin…ôŇčan ku siba ¶uaj ku badańßuŇč a r~…ĺuma Ē~ °. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika a sign is an obj…ô ¶t, *uar~…ĺitj, …ô*…ônt, or …ôntitj wńßos…ô pr…ôs…ôn ¶…ô or o ¶ ¶urr…ôn ¶…ô indi ¶at…ôs tńß…ô probabr~…ĺ…ô pr…ôs…ôn ¶…ô or o ¶ ¶urr…ôn ¶…ô of som…ôtńßiŇč …ôr~…ĺs…ô. a naturar~…ĺ sign b…ôars a ¶ausar~…ĺ r…ôr~…ĺation to its obj…ô ¶t*for instan ¶…ô, tńßund…ôr is a sign of storm, or m…ôdi ¶ar~…ĺ sjmptoms signifj a dis…ôas…ô. a ¶on*…ôntionar~…ĺ sign signifi…ôs bj agr…ô…ôm…ônt, as a fur~…ĺr~…ĺ stop signifi…ôs tńß…ô …ônd of a s…ônt…ôn ¶…ô; simir~…ĺarr~…ĺj tńß…ô words and …ô*pr…ôssions of a r~…ĺaŇčuag…ô, as w…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ as bodir~…ĺj g…ôstur…ôs, ¶an b…ô r…ôgard…ôd as signs, …ô*pr…ôssiŇč parti ¶ur~…ĺar m…ôaniŇčs. tńß…ô pńßjsi ¶ar~…ĺ obj…ô ¶ts most ¶ommonr~…ĺj r…ôf…ôrr…ôd to as signs (noti ¶…ôs, road signs, …ôt ¶., ¶or~…ĺr~…ĺ…ô ¶ti*…ôr~…ĺj known as signag…ô) g…ôn…ôrar~…ĺr~…ĺj inform or instru ¶t usiŇč writt…ôn t…ô*t, sjmbor~…ĺs, pi ¶tur…ôs or a ¶ombination of tńß…ôs…ô. tńß…ô pńßir~…ĺosopńßi ¶ar~…ĺ studj of signs and sjmbor~…ĺs is ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd s…ômioti ¶s; tńßis in ¶r~…ĺud…ôs tńß…ô studj of s…ômiosis, wńßi ¶ńß is tńß…ô waj in wńßi ¶ńß signs op…ôrat…ô.
S.FORM: == bacu == / izaw ku bacu nina kilang. siwkay nu bacu bacu sa, napatuzu' ku zuma a kawaw masaheciay saca canacanan a tuutuud. bacu sa kapah tu ku nu sulit kulit suni saca kawih nu lima, pasubana' tu tawan, u canan ku pakatineng nu tademaw. u bacu nu lacul hakay yadah ku patinengay. u ayzaay a subal nu binawlan, yadah ku bacu, caykalecad ku kuma, malecad ku bacu, sisa izaw ku bacu matahkal i kitakitakit. kya matineng ku caykalecad kamuay a tademaw, u canan ku pasubana'ay. u bacu a bana'-kakuziza , u tatengaay a bana' tuway. katuuday ku minanamay. mapabacu mapabacu Ś∑≤ŚĀöŤ®ėŤôü/ mipabacu tu ku luma' nu tumuk. mipabacu mipabacu ŚĀöŤ®ėŤôü/ mipabacu kaku tu laed nu umah. pabacuan pabacuan ŤĘęś®ôÁ§ļŤ®ėŤôüÁöĄŚúįśĖĻ/ pabacuan tu ku luma' ni Kacaw. sibacuay sibacuay śúČŤ®ėŤôüÁöĄ/ katinengan ku sibacuay ku badahung a luma'. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika A sign is an object, quality, event, or entity whose presence or occurrence indicates the probable presence or occurrence of something else. A natural sign bears a causal relation to its object‚ÄĒfor instance, thunder is a sign of storm, or medical symptoms signify a disease. A conventional sign signifies by agreement, as a full stop signifies the end of a sentence; similarly the words and expressions of a language, as well as bodily gestures, can be regarded as signs, expressing particular meanings. The physical objects most commonly referred to as signs (notices, road signs, etc., collectively known as signage) generally inform or instruct using written text, symbols, pictures or a combination of these. The philosophical study of signs and symbols is called semiotics; this includes the study of semiosis, which is the way in which signs operate.
S.PHON: == ba ¶u == / izaw ku ba ¶u nina kir~…ĺaŇč. siwkaj nu ba ¶u ba ¶u sa, napatuzu Ē~ ° ku zuma a kawaw masańß…ô ¶iaj sa ¶a ¶ana ¶anan a tuutuud. ba ¶u sa kapańß tu ku nu sur~…ĺit kur~…ĺit suni sa ¶a kawińß nu r~…ĺima, pasubana Ē~ ° tu tawan, u ¶anan ku pakatin…ôŇč nu tad…ômaw. u ba ¶u nu r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ ńßakaj jadańß ku patin…ôŇčaj. u ajzaaj a subar~…ĺ nu binawr~…ĺan, jadańß ku ba ¶u, ¶ajkar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad ku kuma, mar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad ku ba ¶u, sisa izaw ku ba ¶u matańßkar~…ĺ i kitakitakit. kja matin…ôŇč ku ¶ajkar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad kamuaj a tad…ômaw, u ¶anan ku pasubana Ē~ °aj. u ba ¶u a bana Ē~ °-kakuziza , u tat…ôŇčaaj a bana Ē~ ° tuwaj. katuudaj ku minanamaj. mapaba ¶u mapaba ¶u ****/ mipaba ¶u tu ku r~…ĺuma Ē~ ° nu tumuk. mipaba ¶u mipaba ¶u ***/ mipaba ¶u kaku tu r~…ĺa…ôd nu umańß. paba ¶uan paba ¶uan ********/ paba ¶uan tu ku r~…ĺuma Ē~ ° ni ka ¶aw. siba ¶uaj siba ¶uaj ****/ katin…ôŇčan ku siba ¶uaj ku badańßuŇč a r~…ĺuma Ē~ °. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika a sign is an obj…ô ¶t, *uar~…ĺitj, …ô*…ônt, or …ôntitj wńßos…ô pr…ôs…ôn ¶…ô or o ¶ ¶urr…ôn ¶…ô indi ¶at…ôs tńß…ô probabr~…ĺ…ô pr…ôs…ôn ¶…ô or o ¶ ¶urr…ôn ¶…ô of som…ôtńßiŇč …ôr~…ĺs…ô. a naturar~…ĺ sign b…ôars a ¶ausar~…ĺ r…ôr~…ĺation to its obj…ô ¶t*for instan ¶…ô, tńßund…ôr is a sign of storm, or m…ôdi ¶ar~…ĺ sjmptoms signifj a dis…ôas…ô. a ¶on*…ôntionar~…ĺ sign signifi…ôs bj agr…ô…ôm…ônt, as a fur~…ĺr~…ĺ stop signifi…ôs tńß…ô …ônd of a s…ônt…ôn ¶…ô; simir~…ĺarr~…ĺj tńß…ô words and …ô*pr…ôssions of a r~…ĺaŇčuag…ô, as w…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ as bodir~…ĺj g…ôstur…ôs, ¶an b…ô r…ôgard…ôd as signs, …ô*pr…ôssiŇč parti ¶ur~…ĺar m…ôaniŇčs. tńß…ô pńßjsi ¶ar~…ĺ obj…ô ¶ts most ¶ommonr~…ĺj r…ôf…ôrr…ôd to as signs (noti ¶…ôs, road signs, …ôt ¶., ¶or~…ĺr~…ĺ…ô ¶ti*…ôr~…ĺj known as signag…ô) g…ôn…ôrar~…ĺr~…ĺj inform or instru ¶t usiŇč writt…ôn t…ô*t, sjmbor~…ĺs, pi ¶tur…ôs or a ¶ombination of tńß…ôs…ô. tńß…ô pńßir~…ĺosopńßi ¶ar~…ĺ studj of signs and sjmbor~…ĺs is ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd s…ômioti ¶s; tńßis in ¶r~…ĺud…ôs tńß…ô studj of s…ômiosis, wńßi ¶ńß is tńß…ô waj in wńßi ¶ńß signs op…ôrat…ô.
